YTD404000000064n/a00n/a00


NOTES:

  • 1) Steelhead counts appear higher at Lower Granite Dam because most years a group which migrated into the river system between October and December overwinters below LGR and continues its migration in early March.
  • 2) Unclipped steelhead are a subset of steelhead. Unclipped steelhead are counted separately from hatchery steelhead at Corps dams on the Columbia and Snake Rivers. Unclipped steelhead are identified as fish with an intact adipose fin. However, a small number of hatchery fish may not have had their adipose fin removed.
  • 3) Lamprey and LPS Lamprey columns represent counts from different locations at Bonneville Dam. Lamprey refers to 16-hour direct monitoring and video counts from the main fishway windows. LPS Lamprey refers to 16-hour optical sensor counts from Lamprey Passage Structures (LPS). There are three LPSs at Bonneville Dam: the Bradford Island auxiliary water supply (AWS) LPS, Cascades Island Entrance LPS, and the Washington Shore AWS LPS. LPS Lamprey counts are not a subset of the window counts but represent additional lamprey passage events recorded separately. See COE metadata document for more information. For 24-hour LPS counts, see COE Adult Fish Count Queries 24-Hour Report.
  • The information in this report is preliminary, as some counts may be corrected or changed by the source. Video counts can cause a delay in posting the data to the web, because the staff at the projects have to review the tapes. See tables below for dates of video counting.
  • After a dam stops counting fish for the season, only the total number of fish are shown on this report. See table below for the counting dates.
  • These data are updated periodically throughout the day.
  • The last column " Source " shows where the data was imported from: NWACE is imported from the COE's Daily Fish Reports; USACE is from US Army Corps of Engineers fish count pages; CHPUD is from Chelan PUD; GCPUD is from Grant PUD and DGPUD is from Douglas PUD and DART is from the University of Washington's Data Access in Real Time, WFA is imported from ODFW's Willamette Falls website.
  • For more metadata on adult counts, click here for FPC metadata and here for COE metadata.

    2025-26 Adult Salmon Dam Count Monitoring Dates

    Dam
    Video Monitoring Dates
    Day
    Night
    Direct Monitoring Dates
    Bonneville Dam
    January 1 - March 31, 2026
    December 1, 2026 - December 31, 2026
    May 15 - September 30, 2026
    		 April 1 to November 30, 2026
    The Dalles Dam
    January 1 - end of February 2026
    June 15 - November 30, 2026
    		 April 1 to October 31, 2026
    John Day Dam
    March 1 - March 31, 2026
    November 1 2026 - end of February
    June 15 - September 30, 2026

    McNary Dam
    June 15 - September 30, 2026
    		 April 1 to October 31, 2026
    Ice Harbor Dam
    January 1 - end of February 2026
    		 April 1 to October 31, 2026
    Lower Monumental Dam
    March 1 - March 30 2026
    November 1 - end of February
    		 April 1 to October 31, 2026

    		April 1 to October 31, 2026
    Lower Granite Dam
    March 1 - March 31, 2026

    Wells Dam

    2025-2026 Columbia and Snake River Fishway Outages

    Dam Dates Fishways are Closed
    Bonneville Dam
    • Bradford Island, Oregon Side (December 1, 2025: February 26, 2026)
    • WA Shore (December 1, 2025: February 26, 2026)
    • Cascade Island (December 1, 2025: February 26, 2026)
    The Dalles Dam
    • East Fish Ladder (December 2, 2025: February 28, 2026)
    • North Fish Ladder (February 16, 2026: February 28, 2026)
    John Day Dam
    • North Fish ladder (December 1, 2025: December 30, 2025)
    • South Fish ladder (January 1, 2026: February 26, 2026)
    McNary Dam
    • Washington Shore (January 18, 2026: February 28, 2026)
    • Oregon Shore (January 4, 2026: January 16, 2026)
    Ice Harbor Dam

    • South Shore (January 1, 2026: January 31, 2026)
